code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test06-MAY-2022Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting renal involvement, possibly nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
